source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure compliance with IBM Cloud password requirements

input_row.source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure compliance with IBM Cloud password requirements
input_row.description: A strong password is a very important step towards account security and safety. Passwords should never be shared with anyone, and must follow the strong password requirements as follow. • Password must contain at least one uppercase letter • Password must contain at least one lowercase letter • Password must contain at least one number • Password must contain at least 8 characters • Passwords should not be reused • Password must only contain ASCII characters • Password cannot contain: spaces, or any of these characters- `;:{"?)<> • Ensure the usage of a password meter which coaches user to create a stronger password than the minimum
input_row.rationale: A strong password can help prevent unauthorized access to an IBM Cloud account. Impact: IBM Cloud automatically prevents the usage of any passwords that do not meet password requirements, when the users are created in IBMid system. When enterprises integrate through enterprise federation (using SAML), then enterprise identity system is responsible for enforcing password strength.
input_row.audit: IBM Cloud automatically prevents the usage of passwords that do not meet password requirements, when the users are created in IBMid system. When enterprises integrate through enterprise federation (using SAML), then enterprise identity system is responsible for enforcing password strength.
input_row.remediation: IBM Cloud automatically prevents the usage of any passwords that do not meet password requirements, when the users are created in IBMid system. When enterprises integrate through enterprise federation (using SAML), then enterprise identity system is responsible for enforcing password strength. Default Value: By default, all passwords associated with users in IBMid system must meet these requirements. When enterprises integrate with IBM Cloud through enterprise federation (using SAML), then enterprise identity system is responsible for enforcing password strength. References: 1. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/account?topic=account-ts_logintoibm Additional Information: Per the latest password guidance use of multi-factor authentication (MFA) should be the preferred method of authentication. A password should only be used when MFA is not available.
input_row.impact: IBM Cloud automatically prevents the usage of any passwords that do not meet password requirements, when the users are created in IBMid system. When enterprises integrate through enterprise federation (using SAML), then enterprise identity system is responsible for enforcing password strength.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/account?topic=account-ts_logintoibm Additional Information: Per the latest password guidance use of multi-factor authentication (MFA) should be the preferred method of authentication. A password should only be used when MFA is not available.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control pertains to password compliance for users in the IBMid system or through enterprise federation using SAML. Given the nature of the control and the reference that IBM Cloud automatically enforces these password requirements, it indicates a standard configuration capability of IBM Cloud identity management services. Therefore, this control can be verified using IBM Cloud's IAM services which clearly fall under security and access control capabilities of IBM Cloud.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_iam_user_password_policy_verification

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
- **IBM Cloud API Calls or CLI SDK Methods to Use:** Utilize IBM Cloud IAM APIs or CLI to check user password policy configurations.
- **IBM Cloud CLI Commands:** Use commands such as `ibmcloud iam user-list` and `ibmcloud iam account-settings-auth` to query account settings related to user authentication.
- **Specific Resources to Enumerate:** Review account settings for password policy configurations and settings that enforce compliance with the standard requirements.
- **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:** 
  - Ensure passwords comply with having uppercase, lowercase, numeric characters, and being at least 8 characters in length.
  - Confirm configurations prevent password reuse, ASCII character compliance, and restricted characters as per guidance.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:** 
  - PASS: All user password policies meet the described characteristics, and account settings enforce these requirements.
  - FAIL: If any account or user compliance deviates from the outlined policy requirements.
- **Example:** Use IBM Cloud CLI to run commands that verify the password strength settings at the organization or account level, ensuring the system prevents non-compliant password use. Validate settings against IBM documentation such as "account-ts_logintoibm".

**SOURCE:** CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
